target.name: Embellicine A fragment
target.motivation: Embellicines exhibit potent cytotoxic activity against human cancer cell lines and strongly inhibit TNFα-induced transcriptional activity of NF-κB, making them new leads for anticancer and anti-inflammatory drugs. The detailed structure-activity relationships need to be elucidated through total synthesis. This paper focuses on synthesizing an A/B-cis-fused cyclopenta[b]fluorene (6/5/6/5) fragment.

prior_syntheses.key_strategy: Diels-Alder reaction to construct A/B-cis-fused tricyclic decahydrofluorene skeleton of pyrrocidine A
IMDA reaction for A/B-trans-fused decahydrofluorene skeletons (hirsutellone B)
IMDA reaction for A/B-trans-fused decahydrofluorene skeletons (GKK1032A2)

success_criteria.free_text: First synthesis of an A/B-cis-fused cyclopenta[b]fluorene (6/5/6/5) ring system with complete stereoselectivity.

key_disconnections.bond_description: C-C bonds forming the A/B-cis-fused tetracyclic skeleton
key_disconnections.reasoning: The IMDA reaction is effective for stereoselective construction of A/B-ring systems. To achieve A/B-cis-selectivity, an eight-membered silylene tether was designed to reverse the orientation of the diene moiety towards the β-side of the C-ring, overcoming steric repulsion in the thermodynamically stable transition state (TS-1) that would lead to A/B-trans fusion. Introducing a cyclopentanone at the diene terminus allows simultaneous construction of the tetracyclic skeleton.

alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Would lead to A/B-trans-fused products due to steric repulsion in the transition state (TS-1 vs TS-2).

key_disconnections.bond_description: C-C bond between vinyl iodide and cyclic vinyl stannane
key_disconnections.reasoning: To connect the silylene-bridged vinyl iodide fragment (8) with the cyclic vinyl stannane fragment (9) to form the IMDA precursor (7).
To construct the C-ring intermediate (10) from dienophile (11) and siloxydiene (12) with stereocontrol.

retrosynthetic_strategy.rationale: The overall strategy involves constructing the C-ring first, then elaborating it to incorporate the silylene tether and the vinyl iodide moiety. This fragment is then coupled with a cyclic vinyl stannane to form the IMDA precursor. The key step is the silylene-tethered IMDA reaction to form the A/B-cis-fused tetracyclic core, followed by functional group manipulations on the D-ring to reach the target fragment.

thought.background: Natural organic compounds containing cyclopenta[b]fluorene (6/5/6/5) ring systems are known, including A/B-trans-fused compounds (phomapyrrolidones, ascomylactams) and A/B-cis-fused embellicines. Embellicines A and B show potent cytotoxic activity and inhibit NF-κB, making them potential drug leads. Prior work by Kobayashi et al. showed A/B-cis-fused tricyclic decahydrofluorene synthesis via Diels-Alder, but required many additional reactions for functional group transformations. Our group previously achieved A/B-trans-fused decahydrofluorene skeletons using IMDA.
thought.gap: The main challenge is to construct an A/B-cis-fused ring system for embellicines, which requires reversing the stereoselectivity of the IMDA reaction from A/B-trans-selective to A/B-cis-selective.
thought.decision: To achieve A/B-cis-selectivity in the IMDA reaction, a tethered structure will be introduced into the IMDA precursor. Specifically, an eight-membered silylene tether will connect two hydroxyl groups on the β-side of the C-ring and diene moiety. This tether is expected to force the diene into a conformation that favors the A/B-cis fusion (Tethered TS). A cyclopentanone structure at the diene terminus will enable simultaneous construction of the tetracyclic skeleton.

evidence.text_span:
The most challenging task in the synthesis of tetracyclic fragments of embellicines is constructing an A/B-cis-fused ring system. In 2012, Kobayashi et al. reported the synthesis of an A/B-cis-fused tricyclic decahydrofluorene skeleton of pyrrocidine A5 (Figure 2). They utilized a Diels−Alder reaction between five-membered cyclic enone containing the B/C-ring and a Danishefsky−Kitahara type diene to construct the A/Bcis-fused tricyclic framework. Although this synthetic strategy is reliable for constructing A/B-cis-fused structures, many additional reactions are required for functional group transformations on the A- and C-rings.
By contrast, we previously reported the efficient synthesis of A/B-trans-fused decahydrofluorene skeletons in the total synthesis of hirsutellone B6 and GKK1032A2. 7 In this strategy, the intramolecular Diels−Alder (IMDA) reaction was effectively utilized for the stereoselective construction of the A/B-ring system. To apply a strategy similar to that used for the construction of the tetracyclic skeleton of embellicine A, it is necessary to completely reverse the stereoselectivity of the IMDA reaction from A/B-trans-selective to A/B-cis-selective.
Then we considered the transition state of the IMDA reaction (Scheme 1). In the thermodynamically stable transition state of the IMDA reaction (TS-1), the diene moiety would be distributed to the α-side toward the C-ring because, in the alternative transition state where the diene moiety is located on the β-side (TS-2), non-negligible steric repulsion between a methyl group of the diene moiety and the axial hydrogen on the C-ring should be generated. Therefore, we introduced a tethered structure into the IMDA precursor to reverse the orientation of the diene moiety toward the β-side of the C-ring. Specifically, two hydroxyl groups were introduced on the β-side of the C-ring and diene moiety. If these groups were connected by a silicon atom, the resulting eightmembered tether would play the desired role in the transition state of the IMDA reaction (Tethered TS). Furthermore, introducing a cyclopentanone structure at the terminus of the diene moiety would enable the simultaneous construction of the desired tetracyclic skeleton through the IMDA reaction. Although such Diels−Alder reactions utilizing electrondeficient dienes conjugated with carbonyl groups have rarely been reported,8 we dare to employ this type of reaction precursor considering the following modifications of the Dring.

thought.background: The C-ring fragment is needed for the overall synthesis. Chiral dienophile 11 can be synthesized from L-ascorbic acid. Siloxydiene 12 can be prepared from (E)-2-methyl-2-pentenal 13.
thought.gap: How to construct the C-ring intermediate 10 with the correct stereochemistry.
thought.decision: Perform an asymmetric Diels-Alder reaction between dienophile 11 and siloxydiene 12, promoted by BF3·OEt2, followed by diastereoselective hydrogenation. This should yield cyclohexene 14 with a β-configured siloxy group, crucial for the tethered structure, and then the desired C-ring intermediate 10.

outcome.observation: The reaction proceeded stereoselectively, yielding cyclohexene 14 with a β-configured siloxy group.

evidence.text_span: The construction of the C-ring was investigated first (Scheme 3). Chiral dienophile 11 was synthesized from Lascorbic acid in a four-step reaction using a reported procedure.9 Siloxydiene 12 was prepared by treating commercially available (E)-2-methyl-2-pentenal 13 with TBSCl and NaI in the presence of Et3N . Resulting dienophile 11 and diene 12 underwent an asymmetric Diels−Alder reaction promoted by BF3·OEt2 . The reaction proceeded stereoselectively, and cyclohexene 14 possesses a β-configured siloxy group, which is crucial for constructing the designed tethered structure.

thought.background: Cyclohexene 14 was obtained with the correct stereochemistry.
thought.gap: Need to hydrogenate the olefin moiety to obtain the C-ring intermediate 10.
thought.decision: Perform diastereoselective hydrogenation of 14 using Pd/C and H2.

outcome.observation: The desired C-ring intermediate 10 was successfully obtained.

evidence.text_span: After the diastereoselective hydrogenation of 14, the desired C-ring intermediate 10 was successfully obtained.

thought.gap: Need to convert the methyl ketone moiety into a propargyl alcohol (15) for subsequent hydrostannylation and iodination.
thought.decision: Convert the methyl ketone of 10 into an enol phosphate, followed by elimination and hydroxymethylation.

outcome.observation: Propargyl alcohol 15 was obtained.

evidence.text_span: The methyl ketone moiety of C-ring intermediate 10 was converted into enol phosphate,10 and the subsequent elimination11 and hydroxymethylation were conducted to afford propargyl alcohol 15.

thought.gap: Need to remove the TBS group to expose the hydroxyl group for subsequent steps.
thought.decision: Treat 15 with TBAF.

outcome.observation: TBS group was removed, yielding 16.

evidence.text_span: After removal of the TBS group, hydrostannylation of the alkyne moiety,12 and subsequent iodination, nontethered vinyl iodide 18 was obtained.

thought.gap: Need to perform hydrostannylation of the alkyne moiety.
thought.decision: Perform hydrostannylation using PdCl2(PPh3)2 and Bu3SnH.

outcome.observation: Hydrostannylation was successful, yielding 17.

evidence.text_span: After removal of the TBS group, hydrostannylation of the alkyne moiety,12 and subsequent iodination, nontethered vinyl iodide 18 was obtained.

thought.gap: Need to convert the stannane into a vinyl iodide.
thought.decision: Perform iodination using I2.

outcome.observation: Nontethered vinyl iodide 18 was obtained.

evidence.text_span: After removal of the TBS group, hydrostannylation of the alkyne moiety,12 and subsequent iodination, nontethered vinyl iodide 18 was obtained.

thought.background: Nontethered vinyl iodide 18 is available. The primary hydroxyl group needs protection, and the dienophile moiety needs to be constructed.
thought.gap: Need to protect the primary hydroxyl group of 18 and then remove the acetonide group.
thought.decision: Protect the primary hydroxyl group of 18 with a TBDPS group to give 19. Then, remove the acetonide group of 19 using propanedithiol and BF3·OEt2 to yield the 1,2-diol.

outcome.observation: The primary hydroxyl group was protected, and the acetonide was removed to give the 1,2-diol (intermediate between 19 and 20).

evidence.text_span: The primary hydroxyl group of 18 was protected with a TBDPS group, and the construction of the dienophile moiety was started. The acetonide group of 19 was removed by a treatment of propanedithiol and BF3·OEt2,13 and the resulting 1,2-diol was once more protected with a p-methoxybenzylidene group.

thought.gap: Need to protect the 1,2-diol with a p-methoxybenzylidene group.
thought.decision: Protect the 1,2-diol with a p-methoxybenzylidene group to yield 20.

outcome.observation: The 1,2-diol was protected with a p-methoxybenzylidene group, yielding 20.

evidence.text_span: The acetonide group of 19 was removed by a treatment of propanedithiol and BF3·OEt2,13 and the resulting 1,2-diol was once more protected with a p-methoxybenzylidene group.

thought.gap: Need to protect the remaining secondary hydroxyl group on the C-ring.
thought.decision: Protect the secondary hydroxyl group of 20 with a TES group to yield 21.

outcome.observation: The secondary hydroxyl group was protected with a TES group, yielding 21.

evidence.text_span: The remaining secondary hydroxyl group on the C-ring of acetal 21 was protected with a TES group, and reductive cleavage of the p-methoxybenzylidene acetal using DIBAL yielded alcohol 23.

thought.gap: Need to reductively cleave the p-methoxybenzylidene acetal.
thought.decision: Perform reductive cleavage of the p-methoxybenzylidene acetal using DIBAL to yield alcohol 23.

outcome.observation: Reductive cleavage of the p-methoxybenzylidene acetal yielded alcohol 23.

evidence.text_span: The remaining secondary hydroxyl group on the C-ring of acetal 21 was protected with a TES group, and reductive cleavage of the p-methoxybenzylidene acetal using DIBAL yielded alcohol 23.

thought.gap: Need to oxidize the primary hydroxyl group to an aldehyde.
thought.decision: Perform Swern oxidation of the primary hydroxyl group of 23 to yield aldehyde 24.

outcome.observation: Swern oxidation of the primary hydroxyl group of 23 yielded aldehyde 24.

evidence.text_span: After Swern oxidation of the primary hydroxyl group of 23, resulting aldehyde 24 was converted into (Z)-configured α,β-unsaturated ester 25 by the Horner− Wadsworth−Emmons (HWE) reaction using the Stille− Gennari reagent.14

thought.gap: Need to convert aldehyde 24 into a (Z)-configured α,β-unsaturated ester.
thought.decision: Perform Horner-Wadsworth-Emmons (HWE) reaction using the Stille-Gennari reagent ((CF3CH2O)2P(O)CH2CO2Me) and KHMDS with 18-crown-6 to yield 25.

outcome.observation: Aldehyde 24 was converted into (Z)-configured α,β-unsaturated ester 25.

evidence.text_span: After Swern oxidation of the primary hydroxyl group of 23, resulting aldehyde 24 was converted into (Z)-configured α,β-unsaturated ester 25 by the Horner− Wadsworth−Emmons (HWE) reaction using the Stille− Gennari reagent.14

thought.background: α,β-unsaturated ester 25 is available. The TES and TBDPS groups need to be removed to expose the hydroxyl groups for silylene tether formation.
thought.gap: Need to selectively remove the TES and TBDPS groups.
thought.decision: Treat 25 with TBAF to simultaneously remove the TES and TBDPS groups, yielding diol 26.

outcome.observation: The TES and TBDPS groups were simultaneously removed, giving diol 26.

evidence.text_span: Finally, an eight-membered silylene tether was introduced. The TES and TBDPS groups of α,β-unsaturated ester 25 were simultaneously removed by treatment with TBAF, giving diol 26.

thought.background: Diol 26 is available. The two hydroxyl groups are in the correct positions for forming the silylene tether.
thought.gap: Need to introduce the eight-membered silylene tether.
thought.decision: Treat diol 26 with Et2NPh2SiCl under basic conditions, followed by gradual addition of DMAP, to form the silylene-bridged vinyl iodide 8.

outcome.observation: The desired eight-membered silylene-bridged vinyl iodide 8 was obtained in high yield.

evidence.text_span: Diol 26 was then treated with Et2NPh2SiCl under basic conditions,15 followed by the gradual addition of DMAP, resulting in the desired eight-membered silylene-bridged vinyl iodide 8 in high yield.16

thought.background: Silylene-bridged vinyl iodide 8 is available, and cyclic vinyl stannane 9 is independently prepared.
thought.gap: Need to couple these two fragments to form the cyclization precursor 7.
thought.decision: Perform a Stille coupling reaction between 8 and 9 using Pd(PPh3)4 and CuTC.

outcome.observation: Cyclization precursor 7 was obtained in good yield.

evidence.text_span: Thus, the designed eight-membered silylene tether was successfully introduced, and a tetracyclic skeleton was constructed (Scheme 5). A Stille coupling reaction between silylene-bridged vinyl iodide 8 and independently prepared cyclic vinyl stannane 9 was conducted to obtain cyclization precursor 7. 17
evidence.section: Construction of the A/B-cis-Fused Tetracyclic Skeleton

thought.background: Cyclization precursor 7 is available, containing the silylene tether and the diene/dienophile system.
thought.gap: Need to perform the key intramolecular Diels-Alder (IMDA) reaction to form the A/B-cis-fused tetracyclic compound.
thought.decision: Heat precursor 7 in xylene to induce the IMDA reaction.

outcome.observation: The expected IMDA reaction proceeded smoothly, producing the desired A/B-cis-fused tetracyclic compound 6 in good yield with complete stereoselectivity. The A/B-cis-fused structure was elucidated by NOESY correlation. The carbonyl group conjugated with the diene moiety did not inhibit the IMDA reaction.

evidence.text_span: Upon heating to 140 °C in xylene, the expected IMDA reaction proceeded smoothly, producing the desired A/ B-cis-fused tetracyclic compound 6 in good yield with complete stereoselectivity. The A/B-cis-fused structure of compound 6 was elucidated by the NOESY correlation. It is noteworthy that the carbonyl group conjugated with the diene moiety of precursor 7 did not inhibit the IMDA reaction.
evidence.section: Construction of the A/B-cis-Fused Tetracyclic Skeleton

thought.gap: Need to convert the cyclopentanone moiety into an enone.
thought.decision: Convert the cyclopentanone moiety of 6 into enone 27 via α-selenenylation, followed by oxidative elimination.

outcome.observation: The cyclopentanone moiety of 6 was converted into enone 27.

evidence.text_span: Next, deoxygenation of the silylene moiety and elaboration of the D-ring were investigated (Scheme 6). The cyclopentanone moiety of 6 was converted into enone 27 via α- selenenylation, followed by oxidative elimination.
evidence.section: Synthesis of the Tetracyclic Skeleton of Embellicines

thought.gap: Need to add a methyl group to the enone in a stereoselective manner.
thought.decision: Perform conjugate addition of a methyl group to enone 27 using the Gilman reagent (Me2CuLi).

outcome.observation: Conjugate addition of a methyl group to enone 27 proceeded with complete stereoselectivity, yielding β-methylketone 28.

evidence.text_span: Conjugate addition of a methyl group to enone 27 using the Gilman reagent proceeded with complete stereoselectivity. Resulting β- methylketone 28 was further methylated at the α-position by successive treatment with LDA and methyl iodide.
evidence.section: Synthesis of the Tetracyclic Skeleton of Embellicines

thought.gap: Need to further methylate the α-position.
thought.decision: Methylate 28 at the α-position by successive treatment with LDA and methyl iodide to yield bismethylated intermediate 29.

outcome.observation: Bismethylated intermediate 29 was obtained.

evidence.text_span: Resulting β- methylketone 28 was further methylated at the α-position by successive treatment with LDA and methyl iodide.
evidence.section: Synthesis of the Tetracyclic Skeleton of Embellicines

thought.gap: Need to cleave the silylene tether to obtain the diol.
thought.decision: Cleave the silylene tether of 29 using HF-pyridine to obtain diol 30.

outcome.observation: Cleavage of the silylene tether yielded diol 30.

evidence.text_span: Cleavage of the silylene tether of bismethylated intermediate 29 was achieved using HF-pyridine to obtain diol 30.
evidence.section: Synthesis of the Tetracyclic Skeleton of Embellicines

thought.background: Diol 30 is available. The two hydroxyl groups need to be removed.
thought.gap: Need to deoxygenate the first hydroxyl group.
thought.decision: Perform Barton-McCombie deoxygenation on one of the hydroxyl groups of 30. First, convert the hydroxyl group to a xanthate using NaH, CS2, and Mel to yield 31.

outcome.observation: One hydroxyl group was converted to a xanthate, yielding 31.

evidence.text_span: The two resulting hydroxyl groups were sequentially removed by repeated Barton−McCombie deoxygenation to afford compound 34.
evidence.section: Synthesis of the Tetracyclic Skeleton of Embellicines

thought.gap: Need to deoxygenate the xanthate.
thought.decision: Perform radical deoxygenation using AIBN and Bu3SnH to yield 32.

outcome.observation: Deoxygenation of the first hydroxyl group was successful, yielding 32.

evidence.text_span: The two resulting hydroxyl groups were sequentially removed by repeated Barton−McCombie deoxygenation to afford compound 34.
evidence.section: Synthesis of the Tetracyclic Skeleton of Embellicines

thought.background: Compound 32 has one remaining hydroxyl group.
thought.gap: Need to deoxygenate the second hydroxyl group.
thought.decision: Repeat the Barton-McCombie deoxygenation. First, convert the hydroxyl group to a xanthate using NaHMDS, CS2, and Mel to yield 33.

outcome.observation: The second hydroxyl group was converted to a xanthate, yielding 33.

evidence.text_span: The two resulting hydroxyl groups were sequentially removed by repeated Barton−McCombie deoxygenation to afford compound 34.
evidence.section: Synthesis of the Tetracyclic Skeleton of Embellicines

thought.gap: Need to deoxygenate the xanthate.
thought.decision: Perform radical deoxygenation using AIBN and Bu3SnH to yield 34.

outcome.observation: Deoxygenation of the second hydroxyl group was successful, affording compound 34.

evidence.text_span: The two resulting hydroxyl groups were sequentially removed by repeated Barton−McCombie deoxygenation to afford compound 34.
evidence.section: Synthesis of the Tetracyclic Skeleton of Embellicines

thought.background: Compound 34 is available. The cyclopentanone moiety needs to be converted into an enol triflate.
thought.gap: Need to form the enol triflate.
thought.decision: Convert the cyclopentanone moiety of 34 into enol triflate 35 using LDA and Comins' reagent.

outcome.observation: The cyclopentanone moiety was converted into enol triflate 35.

evidence.text_span: Finally, the cyclopentanone moiety of this compound was converted into enol triflate 35, and palladium-catalyzed hydrogenolysis was conducted to obtain the desired tetracyclic fragment 5.
evidence.section: Synthesis of the Tetracyclic Skeleton of Embellicines

thought.gap: Need to perform hydrogenolysis to obtain the desired tetracyclic fragment 5.
thought.decision: Perform palladium-catalyzed hydrogenolysis using Pd(OAc)2, P(Cy)3, Et3N, and HCOOH. Electron-rich phosphine ligands are essential.

outcome.observation: Palladium-catalyzed hydrogenolysis was successful, yielding the desired tetracyclic fragment 5. The NOE correlations observed for compound 5 were consistent with those of embellicine A.

evidence.text_span: Finally, the cyclopentanone moiety of this compound was converted into enol triflate 35, and palladium-catalyzed hydrogenolysis was conducted to obtain the desired tetracyclic fragment 5. Interestingly, the use of electron-rich phosphine ligands, such as tricyclohexylphosphine or tributylphosphine, was essential for the reaction to proceed.18 The NOE correlations observed for the tetracyclic skeleton of compound 5 were consistent with those of embellicine A.
evidence.section: Synthesis of the Tetracyclic Skeleton of Embellicines

validation.significance: This work achieved the first synthesis of an A/B-cis-fused cyclopenta[b]fluorene (6/5/6/5) fragment of embellicines via an IMDA reaction of an eight-membered silylene-tethered precursor with complete stereoselectivity. This confirms that embellicines possess an A/B-cis-fused tetracyclic skeleton, resolving previous structural confusion in the field. This is a crucial step towards the total synthesis of embellicine A.

source.title: Synthesis of an A/B-<i>cis</i>-Fused Cyclopenta[<i>b</i>]fluorene (6/5/6/5) Ring System for Embellicine A via the Eight-Membered Silylene-Tethered IMDA Reaction.
source.authors: Sakai Y, Murata R, Hirakawa A, Nakatani Y, Maeda T, Kuwano Y, Morita S, Uchiro H.
